(3/29/2026 14:40 MST) SIGN INFORMATION: Mathew DO,Alvin (3/29/2026 15:39 MST) History and Physical PATI ENT: ROMO, ANNETTE DOB: 03/25/1972 MEDI CAL RECORD #: 875005599 ACCOUNT NUMBER: 501433125 DATE OF ADM SSI ON: 03/28/2026 DATE OF SERVI CE: 03/29/2026 REFERRI NG PHYSI a AN: MD Sumedi ah Forsac MD ADM TTI NG PHYSI a AN: DO Royce Sumayo DO This is a 54-year-old complaining of fall. HI STORY OF PRESENT LLNESS: This is a 54-year-old with past medical history of end-stage renal disease on dialysis Monday, Wednesday, Friday, coronary artery disease and stent placement x5 in December. Presented to the ED after another fall at home. Recently admitted to an outside facility for nosebleed and fall. She was stabilized, resumed on her Plavix and aspirin. She went on the toilet. When she tried to get up, she fell. Then was having left leg pain. Unable to anbul ate. She could not go to dialysis, missed dialysis on Wednesday. CT of the left Lower extremity did not show any acute fractures or findings. The patient's labs showed hyperkal a. Treated in the ED. She was admitted for continued dialysis and treatments. PAST MEDI CAL HI STORY: End-stage renal disease on HD. Coronary artery disease and stents x5. Hypotension at times. Hyperlipidemia. ALLERGI ES: PENI allin FAM LY HI STORY: Noncontributory to the current presentation. SOCI AL HI STORY: She denies tobacco, al cohol , illicit drug use. REVI EW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point systems reviewed and negative except as detailed in HPI. Family history is noncontributory to the current presentation. Report Request ID: 651119643 Page 3 of 5 Print Date/Time: 4/1/2026 18:47 CDT Ensocare Notification ID: 4902983 Apr 01, 2026 05:47:59 PM MDT Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Scottsdale Patient: ROMO, ANNETTE MRN: 875005599 Admit Date: 3/28/2026 FIN#: 501433125 Discharge Date: DOB/Age/Gender: 3/25/1972 54 years Female Attending Phys: Buckner II M.D., James Willis History and Physical MEDI CATI ONS: On admission include Tylenol, Aspirin, Atorvastatin, Plavix, Brimonidine, Opht hal mi c, Diclofenac gel, Folic acid, Metoprolol, M dodrine, Ti mol ol . A 12- poi systems reviewed and negative except as detailed in HPI. PHYSI CAL EXAM NATI ON: VI TAL SI GNS: Blood pressure of 127/58, pulse 79, respirations 18, temperature is 97. 8. Sats 96% on 2 liters. GENERAL: This is a well-nourished, well devel oped female, no acute distress. HEENT: Pupils equal, round, reactive to light and accompdation. Extraocul ar movements intact. Micous membranes are pink and moist. NECK: Supple, no JVD or thyromegaly. HEART: Normal S1 and S2. LUNGS: a ear to auscultation bilaterally. ABDOMEN: Soft, obese, ntender EXTREM TI ES: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. NEUROLOGI C: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves 11-XII grossly intact. No other focal neurological signs. LABORATORY DATA: we do have some I aboratory val ues: Sodi um 136, chloride 99, CO2 23, BUN 87, creatinine 11, alk phos 132, glucose 125, A1c 5.3. ASSESSMENT AND PLAN: 1. Fall, left leg pain. CT is showing no fractures. Continue work with PT/OT, pain control. PM consult. 2. End-stage renal disease. The patient will continue on hemodi ysis Monday, Wednesday, Friday. SKI has been consulted. 3. Coronary artery disease, history of prior PCI s x5 per the notes. Continue on dual antiplatelet therapy and statin therapy. The patient also on metoprolol. 4. The patient having dodrine for the daily for lower ood pressures, probably related todialysis and dialysis treatments. Continue to this medication as needed for her blood pressures. 5. The patient on dual antiplatelet therapy. Working with therapy should help with DVT prophylaxis and treatment. 6. Obesity class III. Continue working with lifestyle modifications.
